channel.title: Frontion — Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from global and regional sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Scrambles for Air Defense Alternatives as Patriot Missiles Run Low
item.description: Russia fired 34 ballistic missiles at Ukraine in a single overnight attack on Kyiv that set the historic Pechersk Lavra ablaze. With Patriot interceptors scarce and increasingly in demand globally, Kyiv is pursuing aging PAC-2/PAC-3 stockpiles and alternative systems to fill the gap.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Can Washington Force Israel to Comply With a US-Iran Peace Deal It Rejects?
item.description: A CBC analysis examines whether the US can compel Israel to withdraw from Lebanon as part of the Iran deal framework. Iranian officials insist Lebanon is covered by the agreement and that Israeli forces must leave, but Israel has not been ordered to withdraw.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Zelensky Proposes Meeting Putin in the US After Russian Leader Ignores G7 Invitation
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelensky said he offered to meet Putin in the United States during a call with Trump, suggesting Putin would find a US meeting difficult to refuse. The proposal came after the Russian leader reportedly ignored a G7 summit invitation.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK to Ban Social Media for Under-16s, May Impose Overnight Curfews
item.description: The UK government announced it will ban social media for children under 16, requiring age verification and potentially imposing overnight curfews on platform access. Platforms including TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube are in scope.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Philippines’ Marcos and Russia’s Putin to Discuss Energy and Food Security
item.description: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. and Russian President Vladimir Putin are set to hold talks focused on energy cooperation and food security, a notable diplomatic engagement as Manila balances ties between Washington and Moscow.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russian strike damages historic Kyiv cathedral, kills 11 across Ukraine
item.description: A major Russian overnight barrage of missiles and drones killed at least 11 people across Ukraine and set fire to the nearly 1,000-year-old Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, one of the country's most sacred cultural landmarks. Ukraine's Orthodox leader called Putin "Antichrist" for the attack.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US and Iran sign framework deal with 60-day ceasefire, leaving major issues for future talks
item.description: The United States and Iran signed an initial framework agreement that includes a 60-day ceasefire to pave the way for negotiations toward a final peace deal and discussions on Iran's nuclear program. Key details on the Strait of Hormuz reopening and sanctions relief remain unresolved.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu faces internal crisis as Trump's Iran deal sidelines Israel
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu huddled with his security cabinet in a bunker as Trump's US-Iran deal left Israel out of the negotiations. The deal does not require Israel to withdraw from Lebanon, and Israeli officials face growing domestic anger over being sidelined.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇺🇸 Macron refuses to scrap France's digital tax, Trump threatens 100% wine tariffs at G7
item.description: French President Macron said France will not bow to US pressure to drop its digital services tax on American tech giants, just hours before meeting Trump at the G7 summit. Trump reportedly threatened 100% tariffs on French wines if the tax isn't killed.

item.title: ⚔️ Initial deal to end US-Iran war moves toward formal signing despite lingering questions
item.description: The US-Iran agreement to extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait of Hormuz is advancing toward formal signing, but critical questions remain about enforcement, Iran's nuclear program, and the role of Israel and regional allies in the broader settlement.

item.title: 🇫🇷🇪🇺🛢️ Macron Tells Trump European Forces Ready to Deploy in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: At the G7 summit opening in Evian-les-Bains, French President Macron told Trump that European naval forces are prepared to help secure the Strait of Hormuz once the US-Iran deal is signed.

item.title: 🇺🇸🛢️🏛️ Trump Says US Won't Need Much Help Reopening Hormuz
item.description: President Trump told G7 allies the United States would not need "much help" in reopening the Strait of Hormuz, downplaying European offers of naval assistance for the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇨🇩⚕️⚠️ Congo Reports Record Daily Increase in Ebola Cases as Outbreak Escalates
item.description: Congolese authorities reported one of the highest daily jumps in Ebola cases since the outbreak was declared, as the virus spreads rapidly beyond quarantine zones into private healthcare facilities.

item.title: ⚽🇨🇩🏥 Ebola Risk for World Cup Extremely Low but US Hospitals Ready, Experts Say
item.description: US infectious disease experts say the risk of an Ebola case arriving during the 2026 World Cup is low but not zero, and American hospitals are prepared to respond if needed.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Says It Repelled Israeli Force Advancing in Southern Lebanon
item.description: Hezbollah announced it repelled an Israeli force attempting to advance in southern Lebanon, raising fears of escalation along the Israel-Lebanon border despite the broader Iran ceasefire.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Parliament to Investigate Its Most Pro-Russian Member
item.description: A special European Parliament committee is set to investigate Fernand Kartheiser, a Luxembourger MEP, for pro-Russian ties, highlighting internal EU tensions over Russia influence.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Trump Threatens 100% Tariff on French Wines Over Digital Tax on Tech Firms
item.description: President Trump threatened a 100% tariff on French wines in response to France imposing a digital services tax on US tech companies, escalating trade tensions at the G7 summit in France.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japanese shippers await mine clearance details before resuming Hormuz transit
item.description: Japanese shipping companies welcomed the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz but say they need details on mine clearance and security guarantees before sending vessels through the chokepoint.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK bans under-16s from social media apps including TikTok and YouTube
item.description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced a ban on social media for children under 16, covering apps like Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube, with the restrictions expected to take effect in early 2027.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France shuts down Israeli company stands at Eurosatory defense exhibition
item.description: A dozen Israeli company stands were shut down at the Eurosatory defense show outside Paris, sparking outrage from Israeli authorities and raising tensions over arms exports during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🛢️ France and Britain push multinational Hormuz naval mission as ceasefire nears
item.description: France and Britain are advancing plans for a multinational naval mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz if a US-Iran ceasefire holds, though it remains unclear whether Tehran would accept any such foreign naval presence.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia slashes fuel quality standards as Ukrainian drone strikes squeeze refineries
item.description: Russia is allowing refineries to produce fuel with lower environmental specifications for the domestic market, Kommersant reports, as Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian energy infrastructure have doubled since the start of 2026, forcing partial shutdowns and supply squeezes.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Erdogan Says Turkey Chose Diplomacy Over Escalation in Iran Conflict
item.description: President Erdogan stated Turkey pursued a measured, diplomacy-first policy during the US-Iran conflict, positioning Turkey as a regional mediator as the Iran deal takes shape.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump, Vance, and Iran Speaker Ghalibaf Electronically Sign Peace Memo
item.description: A senior US official confirmed that President Trump, Vice President Vance, and Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf electronically signed the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with a formal ceremony planned for Friday in Geneva.

item.title: 🌍 G7 Allies Seek Clarity on Iran Deal as Trump Arrives in France
item.description: World leaders gathering at the G7 summit in France pressed for details on the US-Iran memorandum of understanding, with questions remaining about enforcement, the Strait of Hormuz reopening timeline, and Israel-Hezbollah tensions that could threaten the deal.
